mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE crosses 5.0.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicates synchronized weakness across major cyclical demand sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns (t-stat < 1.5) or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth predicts positive returns in <40% of cases during recovery phases or Rank IC turns negative in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening labor demand and higher aggregate income, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns (p > 0.10) across full sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 270.0, elevated inflation expectations trigger anticipated central bank tightening and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 270.0 coincides with positive forward returns in >55% of cases or inflation threshold loses predictive accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic strength and robust consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Combined GDPC1 and PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive returns (interaction coefficient insignificant) or signal reverses during mid-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.5 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential construction and related supply chains, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: HOUST below 1500.0 predicts positive returns in >60% of cases or signal loses predictive power in late-cycle regimes.
